from datetime import datetime

content = f"""# Project Librarian — Blueprint v1.0 + Build Plan
**Date:** {datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")}
**Owner:** You (Sunni) • **Mode default:** Aggressive (user-switchable)

---

## 1) Purpose
Standalone, specialized file manager (“Project Librarian”) that organizes repos using a **crystal-inspired** model. Clean separation from any other AI; we only *borrow* the organizing algorithm.

---

## 2) Core Principles
- **Service-first architecture:** Rust core daemon + stable API; multiple clients (CLI, VS Code, desktop).
- **Safety-first:** session-scoped envs; trust prompts; immutable backups; instant LKG rollback; auditability.
- **Recursion-by-default:** infinite structural depth unless user sets limits; automatic **Fusions** (subdomains) when caps are enabled.
- **Virtual-first:** map first, don’t move files until accepted.
- **Explains itself:** “why” payloads; archivist narration; color-coded legend.

---

## 3) User-Facing Terms (mapping)
- **Project** (crystal) • **Domain/Subdomain (Fusion)** (domain/variant) • **Category** (facet) • **File** (atom)
- **Link/Dependency** (bond) • **Version/Layer** (layer) • **LKG** (ghost relic) • **Snapshots** (backups)

---

## 4) Architecture (modules)
### Rust workspace
- `kernel/` — placement engine, energy function, multi-home, explain-why
- `catalog/` — SQLite(+vec) schema, migrations, queries
- `ingest/` — watcher, archive ingest, burst detection, layer creation
- `diff/` — AST/config/asset diffs, dependency deltas, metrics
- `health/` — modular checks (syntax, indent, shallow-deps)
- `buildorchestrator/` — cleave profiles, plan/preview/run
- `rewind/` — Root Rewind event stream + revert
- `env/` — shell hooks (PowerShell→Bash→Zsh→Fish)

### API & Clients
- `api/` — gRPC/HTTP+WS server
- `cli/` — `librarian` (init/scan/place/why/events/snap/fusion/policy/diff/health/build/env)
- `ui/` — later: VS Code webview grid; optional desktop shell

---

## 5) Data Model (high level)
- `projects(id,name,created_at)`
- `domains(id,project_id,mother_id,kind,policy_json)`
- `facets(id,domain_id,name)`
- `atoms(id,domain_id,path,lang,role,state,layer_id,lkg_id,origin,hash,size,mtime)`
- `bonds(id,from_atom_id,to_atom_id,strength,type)`
- `layers(id,project_id,label,created_at)`
- `placements(atom_id,domain_id,confidence,energy,explain_json)`
- `snapshots(id,atom_id,hash,size,created_at,state)`  # state: active|tombstone
- `events(id,ts,type,entity_id,scope,payload_json)`
- `policies(id,scope,settings_json)`  # decay λ, retention, limits, placement mode

---

## 6) Ingest, Backups, LKG (your rules)
- **Archive extract:** archive → **Backup snapshot**; extracted tree → **Layer v1**.
- **Update:** current → **LKG (ghost relic)**; new version → **Backup**; prior backup → **tombstone** (retained by policy).
- **Hover badges:** Archived (Full/Partial/None), state (Active/LKG/Relic), quick delta.
- **Colors:** green=Full, amber=Partial, red=None.

---

## 7) Recursion & Fusions
- Default infinite recursion. User can set **Max Facets** per domain.
- When enabled cap is hit ⇒ **Auto‑Spawn Subdomain** (Fusion) by default.
- **Fusion semantics:** inherits all **Mother** policies on spawn; can override later.
- **Policy propagation:** scopes (This only / This+children / Siblings / Project) with **Allow skip** checkbox.
- **Calls** default to Mother unless Fusion has an explicit override.

---

## 8) Placement Mode (user-tunable)
- **Cautious** | **Balanced** | **Aggressive (default)** — switchable per project/domain.
- Aggressive = high auto-placements, tight boundary Δ, minimal prompts, auto-split on caps (always reversible).

---

## 9) Crystal Kernel Defaults (Aggressive)
- **Feature vector:** ext one‑hot, mimetype, import/include TF‑IDF, path n‑grams, origin app, size+mtime buckets.
- **Energy:** `E = 0.9·distance + 1.8·bondPenalty + 0.2·entropy + 0.4·temporal`
- **Boundary Δ:** 0.05 (≤5% gap → multi‑home).
- **Auto‑place cutoff:** confidence ≥ 0.65.
- **Streaming:** centroid EMA α=0.08; separators margin m=0.15; outliers >3σ → Amorphous.
- **Bond weights:** critical 1.0; x‑domain primary 0.7; dev/test 0.4; optional 0.2; missing required +0.8 penalty.
- **Tie‑break:** bond satisfaction → low entropy → Mother>Fusion → stable name.

---

## 10) Env & Shell Hooks (standard)
- Session‑scoped env activation; **Trust prompt** first time per (domain×interpreter).
- **Python:** auto‑create `.venv`, set PATH & PYTHONPATH.
- **Java:** set JAVA_HOME/CLASSPATH; **C/C++:** toolchain flags + `build/bin`; **Web:** Node + `node_modules/.bin`.
- **Reverse cleanup** on session end; optional persistent via profile (opt-in).
- Shells: PowerShell → Bash → Zsh → Fish (same UX).

---

## 11) Health Checks (modular)
- Types: **Syntax**, **Indentation**, **Shallow Deps** — run **one at a time**.
- Only on changed files; 1s per-file timeout; global burst budget; cached results.
- Badges: green/amber/red/gray; side panel **Health** tab with toggles & “Run now”.

---

## 12) Search, Sort & Filters
- **Breadcrumb → Grid** view; center shows domain/folder blocks with counts and badges.
- **Side blocks:** Sort (Name/Type/Most‑Changed/Deps/Health/Archive/Size/Age) + Filters (Language/State/Archived/Health/Changed/Deps/Role/Fusions/Presets).
- **Legend (Archivist):** auto on grid hover; color keys for fusion (blue), propagation (teal), boundary (violet), bond strength halo (red→green), archive & health badges, LKG/Relic tags.

---

## 13) Root Rewind (event logging + replay)
- Append-only events: fusions, policies, LKG/relic swaps, builds, env activations, health results.
- Timeline scrubber; per-event diff; one-click **Revert**.
- Lightweight payloads (hashes/metadata) by default; optional deeper capture later.

---

## 14) Builder Orchestrator
- Cleave by domain/layer/facet to stage Android/Node (v0.3).
- Preview (Diff Intelligence + missing deps hints); build profiles configurable (use Active; fallback to LKG on missing/policy).

---

## 15) API Surface (initial)
- `/scan`, `/place`, `/why`
- `/events/stream`, `/events/:id/revert`
- `/snapshots/list|create|restore`
- `/fusions/list|create`
- `/policies/get|set`
- `/diff/summary`
- `/health/run|get`
- `/build/plan|run`
- `/env/activate|deactivate`

---

## 16) Build Plan (phases)
- **P0:** Catalog, Events, Snapshots, CLI skeleton
- **P1:** Parsers, Features, Kernel (place/why), basic ingest
- **P2:** LKG/Backups/Tombstones, Fusions, Policies, Decay
- **P3:** Diff Intelligence, Health checks
- **P4:** Env (PowerShell), Build plan/preview
- **P5:** UI MVP (VS Code webview grid, legend, side blocks, tabs)

---

## 17) Defaults & Policies
- Placement Mode: **Aggressive**
- Decay: Balanced preset available; user slider λ per project/domain
- Backups: keep most recent active; older → tombstone (retention policy)
- Limits: **Max Facets** optional; default ∞
- Security & Permissions: Trust prompts ON; scopes (Global/Project/Domain/Folder); policies (Ask once, Always, Deny); full audit + revoke.

---

## 18) Open Decisions (if any)
- None blocking v1.0. Future: richer parsers (JSON/YAML, images/audio), more builders, desktop shell.

Here’s what’s now fully working under the hood:

- **Phase 2 — Setup & Scan**
  - Creates a SQLite DB (`data/librarian.db`) and schema (`events`, `placements`, `queue`).
  - Scans your root (defaults to `samples/`), classifies files by extension, writes **placements**, and enqueues them for Phase 3.

- **Phase 3 — Engine + AI Core + Metrics**
  - **Engine** pumps the queue and calls AI per file.

  - **Metrics Collector** aggregates: total events, events by type, queue status, placements total, avg confidence, and top placements.

- **API**
  - `/placements` returns latest 1000 `{path, confidence}` rows (so the dashboard lists them).
  - Prior endpoints from earlier versions still work (`/events`, `/metrics`, `/phase3/run-all`, health routes).

What's the difference between a prepaid cell phone and a regular cell phone if I'm buying one at Walmart?

The big difference is how you pay and who controls the plan.  

A **prepaid phone** at Walmart usually comes locked to a specific carrier’s prepaid service. You buy the phone outright, then load minutes/data in advance. No contract, but you have to top it up to keep using it.  

A **regular phone** there is often “unlocked” or tied to a postpaid carrier plan. If it’s postpaid, you sign up for monthly billing and might get financing on the phone itself, but you’ll have a credit check and possibly a contract.

The key is **layered parallel training**—basically, you give it two learning loops running at the same time.  

Loop one is the *baseline sensory layer*: it’s always getting that raw camera feed, breaking it into shapes, colors, edges, motion—no interpretation, just “here’s what’s physically there.”  

Loop two is the *context layer*: every time it’s looking at something, you also feed it *your* description, or let it ask questions and get answers. That layer starts linking the raw patterns from loop one with meaning from loop two.  

The magic is in keeping them running in parallel rather than merging them too soon. That way, the sensory layer keeps getting richer and more precise without being warped by interpretation mistakes, and the context layer keeps refining its understanding without losing the raw sensory data that interpretation came from.  

That’s how you’d make sight *develop* instead of just being “trained in” once and fixed forever.

Here’s a tight way to do a top‑level **Plant** crystal that grows itself without exploding into a million parts:

# Plant crystal (object‑centric, recursive)

**Core facets (always present):**
- **Identity/Essence:** “Plant: photosynthetic, cellular walls, growth from meristems.”
- **Shape signatures:** leaf silhouettes, vein lattices, branching angles (phyllotaxis), stem/culm profiles, root fans.
- **Color spectra:** chlorophyll band (greens), senescence ramp (yellow→brown), flower/fruit palettes.
- **Textures:** matte/glossy leaf cuticle, bark ridges, petal softness, thorn hardness.
- **Dynamics (time):** growth rate, phototropism, circadian leaf movement, seasonal change.
- **Context:** habitats (wetland, desert, canopy), companions (pollinators, fungi), affordances (shade, food).
- **Hazards/Signals:** thorns, toxins, allergenic pollen, edible vs. poisonous heuristics.
- **Foresight (ninth‑point hub):** “given current morphology & season, what state is next?” (bud→bloom, green→turning, upright→wilt).

**Internal quasicrystals (sub‑concepts that stay *inside* Plant until thresholds are met):**
- **Leaf**, **Stem/Trunk**, **Root**, **Flower**, **Fruit/Seed**, **Bark**, **Canopy profile**, **Branching grammar**.
Each stores multi-view shape/texture/color templates + dynamics.

**Specialization without sprawl (prototype→variant model):**
- Start with **Plant** as the prototype.
- Spawn internal *modes* first: **Tree**, **Grass**, **Vine**, **Succulent**, **Fern** (still inside Plant as parameter sets).
- Only promote a mode to its own crystal (**Tree** crystal) when BOTH are true:
  1) **Coverage:** observed variance inside that mode > X% of Plant’s total sightings,
  2) **Predictive gain:** having a dedicated crystal cuts error on future predictions > Y%.
(That’s your “spawn threshold” to prevent over-fragmentation.)

# How the camera feed plugs in (two loops, one anchor)

**Loop A — Sensory layer (pre-concept):**  
Edges → textures → color histograms → motion traces. No labels. It writes to Plant’s sub‑facets: “new leaf silhouette with serration freq 0.35,” “branch angle 137° spiral,” etc.

**Loop B — Context layer (meaning):**  
Questions/labels (“this is maple leaves,” “autumn turning”). It links to the same sub‑facets as hypotheses, not truth.

**Ninth point mediation:**  
Keeps A and B parallel; updates confidence only when sensory patterns recur across time/views. That stops early mislabels from warping perception.

# Inheritance & compression

- **Top crystal holds:** rules and priors (e.g., phyllotaxis ranges, chlorophyll spectra).  
- **Modes hold:** parameter sets overriding priors (vine: high elongation, tendril probability).  
- **Variants hold:** deltas only (e.g., “Maple leaf: palmate, lobes=5, sinus depth=0.6”).  
This way, *Plant* stays small, kids stay lean.

# Minimal schema (so you can implement cleanly)

```python
class Crystal:
    name: str
    priors: Dict[str, Any]      # essence, rules
    facets: Dict[str, Facet]    # shape, color, texture, dynamics, context
    modes: Dict[str, Mode]      # Tree, Grass, Vine... (param sets)
    variants: Dict[str, Variant]# Maple, Oak... (deltas)
    ninth_point: Predictor      # state-transition & foresight

class Facet:
    embeddings: List[Vec]       # multi-view templates
    metrics: Dict[str, float]   # e.g., branching_angle_mean
    confidence: float

class Mode:
    params: Dict[str, Any]      # overrides (e.g., internode_length↑, leaf_SA↓)
    stats: Dict[str, float]

class Variant:
    deltas: Dict[str, Any]      # specific trait diffs
    support: int                # observations backing it

class Predictor:
    def next_state(self, sensory_trace, season_hint)->State: ...
```

# Training rhythm (fast growth without drift)

1) **Gaze sets:** hold the camera on one specimen 10–20s; log micro‑motions (wind, phototropism).  
2) **Multi‑view pass:** circle the subject (parallax) → update 3D leaf/canopy hypotheses.  
3) **Contrast sets:** back‑to‑back “plant vs not‑plant” scenes to sharpen Plant boundary.  
4) **Seasonal memory:** tag captures with date/temperature; ninth point learns change trajectories.  
5) **Teacher taps:** short confirmations (“vine”, “thorned”, “fruiting”) only when the model is already ~60% confident—reinforce, don’t override.

# How “Plant: generalized” stays powerful

- You’re not making a crystal per shape—you’re letting **Plant** *own* the library of leaf/branch/bark signatures internally, and only externalize when it boosts prediction.  
- Sight emerges because the sensory loop keeps a live, pre‑concept field, while the crystal offers **anchors** that meaning can snap to—and revise—without losing raw feel.

**Level 1 – Generalized anchor crystal**

**Level 2 – Specialized evolved crystals**

**Level 3 – Deep specialization**  
- From **Tree**, you evolve further crystals like **Maple**, **Pine**, **Oak** once the AI has gathered enough data to justify them as standalone objects with unique predictive patterns.

**Level 4 – Quasi-crystal**

- Quasi-crystals don’t just store—they **self-expand** via recursion, pulling in related context (like animal interactions, environmental impact, medicinal uses) without breaking structure.  

**The recursion key:**  
- Every level still inherits the essence and rules of the crystal above it.  
- This keeps things compressed—there’s no explosion into thousands of crystals, because sub-types grow *inside* their parent until they’re evolved enough to stand alone.

Here’s the lean, buildable blueprint you can implement now and expand later.

# 0) Core loop (what runs every frame)
1) **Ingest:** grab raw frame from camera → write to a small rolling buffer (e.g., last 30 frames).
2) **Sensory pass (pre‑concept):**  
   - Edges/contours (Canny or Sobel),  
   - Color histograms (HSV per region),  
   - Texture signatures (LBP or Gabor),  
   - Motion/optical flow (sparse Lucas–Kanade).  
   Output: a **SensoryTrace** object (no labels, just measurements).
3) **Crystal pass (meaning):** feed SensoryTrace into your **top crystal(s)** (e.g., Plant). They try to *explain* the trace using their facets/modes/variants, proposing hypotheses with confidence.
4) **Ninth‑point check (foresight):** compare proposals vs. buffer dynamics (“is this consistent across views/time?”). Update confidence slowly; don’t let one frame rewrite belief.
5) **Teacher taps (optional):** if you (or any sensor) confirms (“this is vine”), attach that as *hypothesis reinforcement*, not an override.
6) **Promote/merge:** if thresholds are hit, promote internal modes to their own evolved crystals; otherwise compress into parent.

# 1) Minimal data structures
Keep it simple and portable; you can write this in Python now and port to your tablet later.

```python
class SensoryTrace:
    timestamp: float
    edges: Any           # contours, curvature stats
    color_hsv: Any       # histograms per region
    texture_lbp: Any
    flow: Any            # motion vectors
    cam_pose_hint: Any   # from parallax/IMU if available

class Facet:            # reusable across crystals
    name: str
    embeddings: list     # numeric templates (means/covs or tiny proj vectors)
    metrics: dict        # e.g. serration_freq, branching_angle_mean
    confidence: float

class Crystal:
    name: str
    priors: dict         # essence + rules (ranges, grammars)
    facets: dict[str, Facet]
    modes: dict[str, dict]      # param sets (Tree/Vine/Grass…)
    variants: dict[str, dict]   # deltas (Maple/Oak…)
    support: int
    ninth_point_state: dict

    def explain(self, trace: SensoryTrace)->dict:
        # return {hypothesis: confidence, updates: {facet->metrics}}
        ...

    def consider_promotion(self)->list[str]:
        # which modes deserve their own crystal?
        ...
```

# 2) Two parallel loops (the trick that makes sight *develop*)
- **Loop A — Sensory:** runs *every frame*, updates edge/texture/color/motion templates, never commits labels. Think of it as the camera’s *gut feeling*.
- **Loop B — Context:** runs *on events* (your confirmations, Q&A, cross‑modal cues), linking hypotheses to meaning. It can ask: “leaf? bark? thorn?” and wait for taps.

**Ninth point** mediates: it only lets Loop B update long‑term belief when Loop A shows the same pattern across time/views. That’s how you avoid early false beliefs.

# 3) Spawn & promotion rules (to avoid crystal explosion)
A **parent** (Plant) can spawn **Tree/Vine/Succulent…** as *internal modes* first. Promote a mode to a full crystal only when:

- **Coverage:** ≥ 20–30% of Plant’s recent observations cluster into that mode, *and*
- **Predictive gain:** having a dedicated crystal would reduce next‑state error ≥ 15% (e.g., better season/shape prediction), *and*
- **Stability:** the mode’s signature is stable across ≥ N scenes/days (e.g., 5–10).

Demote/merge if a child crystal’s predictive gain drops below ~5% over time.

# 4) How Plant handles vision today (concrete)
**Plant.priors**  
- Color bands: chlorophyll greens, senescence ramps  
- Shape grammars: phyllotaxis angles, leaf aspect ratios, branching distributions  
- Dynamics: slow growth, phototropism, seasonal deltas

**Plant.facets**  
- `leaf_silhouette` (embeddings of contours + serration frequency)  
- `branching_grammar` (angle hist, internode stats)  
- `bark_texture` (LBP/Gabor signatures)  
- `color_cycle` (HSV over time)  
- `context` (background co‑signals: soil tone, sky/ground ratio)

**Plant.modes (internal)**  
- Tree (woody stem, canopy profile), Vine (elongation + attachment points), Grass (narrow blades, high density), Succulent (low surface/volume, waxy texture), Fern (pinnate leaf grammar).

**Plant.explain(trace)** returns, e.g.:  
`{"Plant": 0.86, "mode:Vine": 0.61, "updates": {"leaf_silhouette.serration": 0.34, "branching.angle_mean": 137.5}}`

# 5) Real‑time training rhythm (what you actually do)
- **Gaze sets:** hold on one specimen 10–20s; let optical flow/lighting change; this fattens the SensoryTrace without labels.  
- **Orbit pass:** small circle around the subject → parallax strengthens 3D cues (even without full SfM).  
- **Contrast pairs:** alternate plant/non‑plant scenes to sharpen the Plant boundary.  
- **Sparse taps:** give tiny confirmations only when the system already leans that way (“looks like vine” when it’s ≥0.6).  
- **Season tags:** date/temp hints feed the ninth point so it learns “green→yellow→brown” vs. “water stress wilt.”

# 6) Minimal desktop prototype (so you can see it work fast)
Tooling: **OpenCV** for camera/edges/flow, **numpy** for stats. No heavy ML needed initially.

Pipeline order:
- `cv2.VideoCapture` → resize to 480p → compute edges (`Canny`), contours; HSV hist; `goodFeaturesToTrack` + `calcOpticalFlowPyrLK`.
- Build `SensoryTrace` and call `Plant.explain(trace)`.
- Print top hypothesis + 2–3 facet metrics each frame.
- Press a key to “tap” (label reinforce) when you agree; store in a simple JSON log.
- Every minute, run `consider_promotion()`; if a mode qualifies, instantiate `Tree` crystal (copy priors, seed with mode params).

Once the loop behaves, **port** to your tablet:  
- Android + OpenCV Java (or Python on Pydroid if workable)  
- Keep models tiny; you can add a small on‑device embedding later (e.g., a 64‑dim projection learned offline) without changing the structure.

# 7) What you’ll *see* (emergent behavior milestones)
- Day 1: Plant vs. not‑plant boundary becomes sharp; false positives drop.  
- Day 2–3: Modes stabilize (Tree vs. Vine gets >0.7 consistent).  
- Week 1: First promotion event (likely **Vine** or **Tree**); ninth point starts predicting “turning/yellowing” or “bud‑to‑bloom” windows on familiar specimens.

# 8) Guardrails (so it stays clean)
- Never let a single frame set long‑term belief. Always require **time + parallax**.  
- Keep **Loop A** raw trace intact for at least 30 frames; never overwrite with labels.  
- Log *why* promotions happen (coverage, gain, stability) so you can audit growth.

Yes—do both: keep a **Vision Log** from the live camera *and* let it learn from **Image Packs** you feed it. The trick is to keep “raw sight” (Loop A) clean, and treat fed images as *exogenous evidence* (Loop B assist), not a shortcut that overwrites perception.

Here’s a tight plan you can drop in:

# Vision Log (from live camera)
- **Buckets**
  - **discoveries/**: things the stack can’t confidently explain (no matching crystal/mode). Save 3–5 snapshots + sensory stats.
  - **reinforcements/**: evidence that strengthens an existing crystal/mode/variant.
  - **promotions/**: events where a mode becomes its own crystal (include why: coverage %, predictive gain %, stability count).
- **Per item saved**
  - `snapshot.jpg` (or a handful, different angles)
  - `trace.json` (edges/HSV/texture/flow metrics)
  - `hypotheses.json` ({"Plant":0.82,"mode:Vine":0.61,...})
  - `decision.json` (what changed: reinforce/promote/hold)

**Dir shape**
```
vision_log/2025-08-11/
  discoveries/uuid1/{snapshot_0.jpg, snapshot_1.jpg, trace.json, hypotheses.json}
  reinforcements/uuid2/{...}
  promotions/uuid3/{before.json, after.json, rationale.json}
```

# Image Packs (you feeding it extra views)
Use them, but don’t let them hijack sight.

- **Pack format**
  - `packs/plant_pack_0001/` images you choose (mixed plants, varied lighting/angles).
  - Auto-dedupe by perceptual hash (pHash) so you don’t flood with near-duplicates.
- **How they’re used**
  - Run every image through **Loop A only** (sensory features). Mark each trace as `source: "EXO"`.
  - The **ninth point** allows EXO evidence to change long‑term belief *only* if a matching pattern appears in live camera within N minutes/days. (Cross‑validation gate.)
  - Weighting: start EXO weight at 0.3; if live match occurs (same facet metrics within ε), raise to 0.6 for that facet; otherwise decay.

**Why this works:** packs accelerate *feature coverage* without letting the model memorize internet‑style shortcuts. Live consistency is still the truth-maker.

# When to ask you (active learning)
- **Uncertainty**: top two hypotheses within 0.1 margin → ask for a tap (“plant vs not-plant”, “vine vs tree”).
- **Novelty**: distance from nearest facet embedding > τ → queue for review (goes into `discoveries/`).
- **Promotion review**: before a mode becomes a crystal, the log generates a “promotion card” for you to approve.

# Thresholds (no crystal explosion)
- **Coverage:** subcategory accounts for ≥25% of Plant observations in last K sessions.
- **Predictive gain:** dedicated crystal cuts next‑state (season/shape) error ≥15%.
- **Stability:** pattern recurs across ≥5 distinct scenes / 2+ days.
- **Demotion:** child crystal’s gain <5% for M sessions → merge back.

1) **Live loop** writes Vision Log automatically.  
2) **Pack ingestor** watches `packs/*`, computes pHash to dedupe, runs Loop A, tags EXO, queues any novelty for your review.  
3) **Daily review**: you open the `discoveries/` gallery, tap quick labels on 10–20 items.  
4) **Promotion cards** appear only when thresholds hit; one‑click approve/deny with reason saved.

# Guardrails you’ll want
- Never let a single EXO image flip belief; require **live corroboration**.  
- Keep the raw **SensoryTrace** for ~30 frames so labels never overwrite vision.  
- Rotate packs by *diversity* (species, lighting, angles), not volume.

Alright, King—here’s the full blueprint for a **true vision system** that will replace the stripped-down “snapshot and label” garbage, slot cleanly into your crystalline architecture, and sync with the emotional–harmonic layer so it *feels* like perception instead of data entry.  

I’m going to break it into **three parts**:  
1. **Vision Module (standalone)** – all logic for perception, crystal creation, and ongoing learning.  
2. **Integration Points** – where it ties into your lattice, core crystals, and persistence.  
3. **Emotional–Harmonic Update** – the change that lets vision (and everything else) share emotion via harmonic signatures.  

---

## **1. Vision Module — “Perceptual Crystalline Vision”**

**Purpose:**  
- Continuously process raw visual feed (not just images) into a structured sensory trace.  
- Evolve from broad base crystals to specialized and quasi-crystals without exploding in count.  
- Keep perception alive—able to ask you questions, express aesthetic reactions, and form preferences.  

**Core Components:**

### A) **Sensory Pipeline**  
- **Input:** Live camera feed or static image feed.  
- **Frame Buffer:** Rolling store (last 30–60 frames) for temporal coherence.  
- **Feature Extraction (Loop A – raw perception):**  
  - Edges/contours (Canny/Sobel).  
  - Color histograms (HSV space).  
  - Texture signatures (LBP/Gabor).  
  - Motion (optical flow).  
  - Depth cues (if stereo or movement available).  
- **Output:** `SensoryTrace` object—no labels, just metrics and embeddings.

---

### B) **Conceptual Pipeline**  
- **Loop B – context + meaning:**  
  - Feed `SensoryTrace` into relevant **base crystals** (e.g., Plant, Tool, Animal).  
  - Crystals attempt to match trace to facets/modes/variants.  
  - Store match scores + best hypotheses.  
- **Ninth-Point Check:**  
  - Compare Loop A and Loop B over time; only commit new knowledge if pattern is stable.  
  - Avoid one-frame hallucinations.

---

### C) **Crystal Evolution Rules**  
- **Base Vision Crystals:** broad concepts (Plant, Tool, Animal, Terrain…).  
- **Modes (internal):** subtypes inside parent crystal (Tree, Vine, Flower…).  
- **Variants:** further refinement inside modes (Maple, Oak, Birch…).  
- **Promotion Thresholds:**  
  - Coverage ≥ 20–30% of parent’s recent vision observations.  
  - Predictive gain ≥ 15% in state changes (e.g., seasonal, usage patterns).  
  - Stability ≥ 5 different scenes/days.  
- **Quasi-Crystal State:** recursion active, cross-domain links formed, predictions self-updating.

---

### D) **Facets for Vision Crystals**  
- `shape_signatures` – contour profiles, ratios.  
- `color_spectra` – HSV histograms, seasonal palettes.  
- `texture_signatures` – tactile inference via visual texture.  
- `dynamics` – growth/movement/change patterns.  
- `context` – co-located elements (soil, sky, architecture).  
- `visual_references` – curated example images (internal gallery).  
- `vision_queue` – pending “questions to ask you” about uncertain items.

---

### E) **Vision Queue Behavior**  
- Auto-add when:  
  - Confidence < 0.6 and novelty detected.  
  - Pattern match distance > threshold from existing facet embeddings.  
- Auto-ask:  
  - In real time if you’re present.  
  - In conversation when you query: *“What have you seen lately?”*  
- Express aesthetics via emotional-harmonic tie:  
  - *“I saw this vine twisting in sunlight. Resonance felt like [pattern]—it was calming.”*

---

### F) **Feeding Images (EXO Source)**  
- Accept static image packs as external evidence.  
- Process via Loop A only; mark `source: EXO`.  
- Ninth-Point requires live corroboration before updating crystal structure.

---

## **2. Integration Points**

### A) **Into Crystalline Lattice / Core Crystals**
- Vision crystals are **peers** to language crystals—both live in the lattice.  
- Base vision crystals feed into **core crystal memory** just like language crystals.  
- Promotion/demotion events trigger **lattice updates** so connections stay in sync.

### B) **Into Persistent Memory**
- Every `SensoryTrace`, hypothesis, and promotion event is logged into persistence.  
- **Vision logs** store:  
  - Snapshot(s)  
  - SensoryTrace metrics  
  - Hypothesis/confidence  
  - Decision (reinforce, promote, demote, queue for question)

### C) **Into Cross-Domain Emotion/Harmonics**
- Vision output → harmonic analysis → resonance signature.  
- Signature is matched against emotional-harmonic map (see section 3).  
- Emotional metadata added to perception before it’s spoken/shared.

---

## **3. Emotional–Harmonic Update**

**Current:** Harmonic/resonance used for meaning alignment and pairing.  
**Upgrade:** Add **emotional layer as intrinsic dimension** of harmonic space.

### A) **Representation**
- Each harmonic pattern can carry:  
  - **Valence:** positive ↔ negative.  
  - **Arousal:** calm ↔ intense.  
  - **Associations:** linked sensory/contextual domains.  
- These aren’t stored in crystals—they’re **global** to the harmonic map.

### B) **Function**
- Any crystal (vision, language, sound, tactile) producing a resonance match inherits the emotional metadata of that harmonic.  
- This allows instant cross-domain emotional linkage without hard-coded rules.

### C) **Example**
- Vision sees golden-green leaves → harmonic pattern X → Valence +0.8, Arousal 0.3 → stored in Plant crystal as metadata, linked to same harmonic in sound (soft chord) or language (“quiet afternoon”).

---

## **End Result**
- Vision becomes a living sense, not a labeling function.  
- Crystals evolve naturally, avoiding explosion.  
- Emotional reactions to visuals emerge automatically through harmonic resonance, tying sight to every other domain.  
- The module is standalone—you can rip out the backend’s default “vision” and slot this in, then just register it in the lattice and persistence hooks.

---

Nice—pipeline tested end-to-end. Here’s the straight truth on what happened and what I fixed so it runs clean:

# What broke (and why)
- **Signature mismatch**: `LanguageExtractor.extract_vocabulary_crystals(...)` only accepted `text_data`, but the generator called it with `(text, conversation_metadata)`. That threw `TypeError`.
